evals.prompt: Let's analyze some competitor websites for our project.
evals.expected_output: Should check existing state (BRIEF.md references, lab/benchmarks/), then ask how the Art Director wants to share references (Figma, URL, Screenshot)

assertions.text: Presents the three input sources: Figma, URL, Screenshot
Assigns progressive IDs (R1, R2, R3) to references

evals.prompt: Add these references to the benchmark: https://example1.com, https://example2.com, https://example3.com
evals.expected_output: Should accept batch input, assign IDs R1-R3, attempt to capture each via Puppeteer with cookie banner dismissal, and ask about annotation mode

assertions.text: Asks about annotation mode (AD-led, Claude-led, or pre-annotated)

evals.prompt: Start a benchmark. There's already a previous benchmark in lab/benchmarks/.
evals.expected_output: Should detect the existing benchmark and ask whether to update, add references, or start over

assertions.text: Asks whether to update, add, or start over
Does NOT overwrite existing benchmark without asking

evals.prompt: Generate the benchmark report. All references have been analyzed.
evals.expected_output: Should generate lab/benchmarks/benchmark-report.md with comparative analysis, pattern map (convergences, divergences, absences), and open doors for CREATIVE_DIRECTION.md

assertions.text: Includes convergences, divergences, and absences sections
Presents Open Doors as exploration space, not recommendations
Does NOT write to CREATIVE_DIRECTION.md without an explicit trigger
